# Figure description: 7.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-II/7.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite geological figure** consisting of three components:
1. A **regional geological map** (1:200,000 scale) — the dominant upper portion
2. Two **geological/geophysical cross-sections** (разрезы) — in the lower portion, labeled "Разрез по линии ОП" (Section along line OP) and "Разрез по линии ФХ" (Section along line FKh)

The cross-sections combine **gravity/magnetic anomaly curves** in the upper panels with **interpreted geological cross-sections** in the lower panels, making them integrated geophysical-geological profiles.

---

## Coverage and Subject

- **Map sheet**: М-42-II (clearly labeled at top), at 1:200,000 scale
- **Region**: Northern Kazakhstan (Казахская ССР), produced by the **Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение** (Central Kazakhstan Production Geological Association), Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR
- **Date**: 1984 г. (1984)
- **Authors**: Составители Смолянова Е.М., Филатова Г.В.; Нармания Б.В., Глухов А.М.
- An **index map** (схема расположения листов) appears in the upper right corner, showing the position of sheet М-42-II relative to neighboring sheets (М-42-I, М-42-VII, etc.)
- A **survey coverage scheme** (Схема геолого-съёмочных маршрутов) is also shown in the upper right, referencing prior surveys by Чепурдов (1939 г.), Дмитров (линия М.В.5, 1971 г.), and others; total area stated as **4094 км²**
- The cross-sections run along two named geophysical survey lines: **ОП** and **ФХ**
- Depth scales on cross-sections reach approximately **−4000 to −5000 m** (subsurface) and elevation profiles span roughly **−150 to +450 m** above sea level
- Geological units visible in cross-sections include symbols such as: **V₂₃, aT, Д³вок, φ₁, φ₂, μ₁С₁, μ₂С₁, D₁₋₂, P₁ₘ, P₁ₐₛ**, and others indicating Vendian through Permian stratigraphy

---

## Key Content

### Geological Map (Upper Panel)
- The map displays a **complex, multi-colored pattern** of geological units representing a wide range of ages from Precambrian/Vendian through Mesozoic/Cenozoic
- **Color scheme** (approximate interpretation):
  - **Pale yellow/cream**: Quaternary and Neogene sedimentary cover
  - **Orange-brown and red-brown tones**: Devonian–Carboniferous volcanic and intrusive rocks (dominant in central and southern portions)
  - **Blue/grey-blue**: likely Ordovician or Silurian sedimentary units
  - **Pink/lilac**: granitic intrusions
  - **Dark brown/maroon**: older metamorphic or volcanic basement
  - **Green patches**: possibly mafic/ultramafic intrusions or specific Paleozoic units
  - **Yellow patches**: possibly Permian or Triassic continental sediments
- The map shows **prominent structural lineaments** (faults), drawn as orange/red lines crossing the map diagonally — these appear to be major regional fault systems with NW-SE and NE-SW orientations
- The central and southern portions of the map are geologically complex with tightly folded and faulted Paleozoic terranes
- The northern portion shows more subdued, flatter-lying younger sedimentary cover (paler colors)
- **Dotted patterns** in northern areas suggest Quaternary or drift-covered terrain
- Several **mineral occurrence symbols** (small colored dots or triangles) are visible, particularly in the central zone

### Cross-Section ОП (Middle Panel)
- **Upper curve**: Gravity anomaly (ΔT or Δg curve) showing significant variations, with a prominent **positive anomaly** (peak ~+400 nT or mGal equivalent) near the right end of the section, and several undulations in the left-center
- **Lower geological panel**: Shows interpreted subsurface structure to approximately **−4000 to −5000 m**
  - A relatively flat **sedimentary cover sequence** (Mesozoic-Paleozoic platform cover) in the upper levels
  - Folded and faulted **Paleozoic basement** below, with color-coded stratigraphic units
  - Significant **vertical displacements** along faults visible in basement structure
  - Pink-colored zones (granites?) visible at depth in certain fault-bounded blocks
  - Units labeled include: **μ₁С₁, μ₂С₁, D₁₋₂, P₁, V₂₃, aT** among others

### Cross-Section ФХ (Lower Panel)
- **Upper curves**: Two overlapping geophysical curves — one showing **ΔZ₁ΔZ₂** (likely vertical magnetic component components) and another (gravity), with scales in **кГл (кГал?)** and **мГл (мГал)**
- The anomaly pattern shows **two distinct positive peaks** on the left side and gentler undulations toward the right
- **Lower geological panel**: Similar depth extent (~−5000 m), showing:
  - Gently dipping **platform cover** at top
  - Deeper folded basement with color-coded units
  - **Vendian (V₂₃)** and **aT (Triassic?)** units visible as labeled horizons
  - The basement surface is irregular, with horst-graben structures implied by the pattern
  - **Pink zones** again visible, likely representing granitic bodies at depth

## Text and Labels

**Header text (verbatim):**
> МИНИСТЕРСТВО ГЕОЛОГИИ КАЗАХСКОЙ ССР
> ЦЕНТРАЛЬНО-КАЗАХСТАНСКОЕ ПРОИЗВОДСТВЕННОЕ ГЕОЛОГИЧЕСКОЕ ОБЪ
